e63_context_policy.context_derivation: V66 E63 repair replay uses sequence plus public non-coordinate RCSB/V65 panel metadata. It separates provider-backed transmembrane topology from hydrophobic-only, cofactor-pocket, oligomeric-interface, and peripheral/monotopic membrane contexts.
reasons: E63 context sees explicit topology-provider transmembrane evidence.

prediction_sources.evidence_statement: Raw amino-acid sequence for the RCSB polymer entity.

RCSB title: CRYSTAL STRUCTURE OF KEX1(DELTA)P, A PROHORMONE-PROCESSING CARBOXYPEPTIDASE FROM SACCHAROMYCES CEREVISIAE. Entity description: KEX1(DELTA)P. Organism: Saccharomyces cerevisiae. Polymer composition: protein/oligosaccharide. Sequence-derived marks: sequence-derived low-complexity tendency high; sequence-derived hydrophobic closure tendency. Coordinates, native contacts, residue-residue distances, and validation annotations are unopened before the prediction hash.

V66 E63 replay metadata context marks: membrane_context_strong transmembrane_context. E63 separates true transmembrane topology from hydrophobic-only, peripheral, cofactor-pocket, and oligomer-interface explanations. Coordinates, contacts, ligand geometry, native topology, and post-seal validation labels are blocked before sealing.
metadata_context_reasons: E63 context sees explicit topology-provider transmembrane evidence.
